MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Defendant D. Zelinsky And Sons, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-16-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Patrick Campbell of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiffs and opposing parties; Christi M .Gotvald of Walsworth LLP (415.781.7072) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Plaintiffs' objections to and motion to strike defendant's supplemental papers filed May 12, 2016 (supplemental amended separate statement, supplemental declaration, and request for judicial notice) are granted. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent James Owen was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Robert Cantley preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs' responses identified Robert Cantley as a person with knowledge, identified what knowledge he has regarding Mr. Owen's exposure to asbestos-containing joint compound and taping mud by defendant's employees, and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Cantley does not have such knowledge. Further, Mr. Cantley testified at his deposition that the mud at issue was asbestos and defendant failed to further address the issue of whether he had foundation to make such statements. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Rhonda Harris, CSR 7414 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)
